articles.title: FreeBSD ate my ram
articles.summary: FreeBSD ate my ram is a phenomenon where the operating system's RAM usage appears to be higher than expected when using tools like fastfetch or btop. This can be attributed to the way FreeBSD manages its RAM, which involves caching data in both RAM and disk storage. The kernel divides physical memory into pages of 4KiB, adds them to queues, and then moves them around based on process needs. When allocated RAM is not being used frequently, it is stored on disk as swap space, but can be retrieved when needed. This cache behavior affects how the operating system reports memory usage, with top reporting active pages in addition to physical memory and swap. As a result, FreeBSD's RAM usage may appear higher than expected due to this caching mechanism.

Understanding these concepts is essential for developers to optimize system performance and troubleshoot issues.

articles.title: Giant trees have no trouble pumping water to top branches: new research
articles.summary: Giant trees have no trouble pumping water to their top branches because they have evolved intricate adaptations that allow them to maintain a constant flow of water even under extreme low pressures, despite being taller than smaller trees. This is due in part to the presence of thin, hollow vessels that suck water upwards and create low pressure at the top of the tree. The study found that these vessels can compensate for the challenges of drawing water to the top by growing wider near the ground and developing leaves that are more resistant to drought stress.

In December 2025 I wrote about my retirement from live streaming , which was once a valuable part of my career in developer relations (DevRel). Since closing that chapter I have redesigned my website , launched a new side project, released some new music , and made a lot of space to reflect on the last five and a half years I’ve spent in DevRel. As I observe the wood pigeons, collared doves, gold crests, house sparrows, robins and a family of squirrels coexist peacefully and peck the seeds and munch on the nuts I become painfully aware of how much has changed in the technology industry throughout that time.
